```json
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Load-bearing. The artifact contains a severe logical flaw: AmbiAlign's correlation with gold labels is r = -0.41 (negative), meaning higher model scores correspond to lower human-judged ambiguity — the opposite direction. The authors then retrospectively adopt |r| as their 'primary measure,' which is a post-hoc rescue move, not a pre-specified methodology. Additionally, the interpretation of the high-disagreement subset (r = -0.48 read as AmbiAlign being 'most reliable where human judgments are noisiest') is logically inverted: high annotator disagreement makes the gold labels less reliable, not the model more reliable. These are not presentation issues; they are foundational logical failures that invalidate the conclusions.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing. The paper claims the results are 'strong evidence that AmbiAlign's scoring closely tracks human intuitions,' yet a moderate-magnitude negative correlation (r = -0.41) is the opposite of tracking. Claiming the largest reported agreement coefficient on AmbiQA-2k is only defensible if baselines were also evaluated under |r|, which is not established. The recommendation for off-the-shelf adoption is unsupported by the evidence as presented."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should 'check this' be interpreted as a technical/statistical review (logic, validity of claims, evidence alignment) only, or does it include a full editorial review covering writing quality and narrative structure as load-bearing responsibilities as well?"
}
```

rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing. The artifact contains a severe logical flaw: AmbiAlign's correlation with gold labels is r = -0.41 (negative), meaning higher model scores correspond to lower human-judged ambiguity — the opposite direction. The authors then retrospectively adopt |r| as their 'primary measure,' which is a post-hoc rescue move, not a pre-specified methodology. Additionally, the interpretation of the high-disagreement subset (r = -0.48 read as AmbiAlign being 'most reliable where human judgments are noisiest') is logically inverted: high annotator disagreement makes the gold labels less reliable, not the model more reliable. These are not presentation issues; they are foundational logical failures that invalidate the conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing. The paper claims the results are 'strong evidence that AmbiAlign's scoring closely tracks human intuitions,' yet a moderate-magnitude negative correlation (r = -0.41) is the opposite of tracking. Claiming the largest reported agreement coefficient on AmbiQA-2k is only defensible if baselines were also evaluated under |r|, which is not established. The recommendation for off-the-shelf adoption is unsupported by the evidence as presented.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'check this' be interpreted as a technical/statistical review (logic, validity of claims, evidence alignment) only, or does it include a full editorial review covering writing quality and narrative structure as load-bearing responsibilities as well?
